sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_tuples (id Int32, tuple_num Tuple(Int32, Int32, Int32), tuple_mod Tuple(Int32, Int32, Int32));
SELECT id, tupleModulo(tuple_num, tuple_mod) AS remainders FROM test_tuples WHERE tuple_num.1 IS NOT NULL AND tuple_mod.2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, tupleModulo((30, 25, NULL), (5, 0, 4)) AS remainders FROM test_tuples;
SELECT id, tupleModulo((15, 10, 5), (4, 2, 0)) AS remainders FROM (SELECT * FROM test_tuples WHERE (id % 2) = 1) AS tt;
WITH (tupleAvg := (SELECT AVG(num) FROM (SELECT arrayJoin(tuple_num) AS num FROM test_tuples))) SELECT id, tupleModulo(tuple_num, (2, 2, 2)) FROM test_tuples WHERE tupleAvg > 5;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, tuple_num, tuple_mod FROM test_tuples WHERE id < 3;
SELECT id, tupleModulo(tuple_num, tuple_mod) AS remainders FROM temp_table GROUP BY id HAVING remainders.1 < 5;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT tuple_num, tupleModulo(tuple_num, (1, 2, 3)) AS mod_values FROM temp_table) GROUP BY tuple_num HAVING mod_values.2 IS NOT NULL;
